google.name: Google
google.focus: Algorithms, Data Structures, System Design, Scalability, and low-level performance analysis.
sampleQuestions: Explain how a hash map handles collisions internally, and discuss the time complexity tradeoffs.
How would you design a distributed rate limiter for a global API servicing millions of requests per second?
Can you walk me through the differences between process-based and thread-based concurrency models?

amazon.name: Amazon
amazon.focus: Customer Obsession, Leadership Principles, Architectural Scalability, AWS cloud design patterns, and ownership.
sampleQuestions: Describe a time when you had to make a quick decision without complete data. What was the impact?
How would you design a high-throughput shopping cart system that handles flash sale workloads on AWS?
What is your approach to handling service failure in microservice architectures? How do you ensure resilience?

microsoft.name: Microsoft
microsoft.focus: OOP principles, systems programming, memory management, Azure integrations, and operating system basics.
sampleQuestions: Explain the concept of memory leaks in managed versus unmanaged languages, and how to debug them.
How would you implement a thread-safe Singleton pattern in a modern programming language?
Design a real-time collaborative document editing system like Microsoft Office Online.

tcs.name: TCS
tcs.focus: Core Java, DBMS/SQL Queries, SDLC models (Agile vs Waterfall), OOPs concepts, and software testing.
sampleQuestions: What is polymorphism in Object-Oriented Programming, and what is the difference between overloading and overriding?
How would you write or explain a query to find the second highest salary from an Employee database table?
Explain the difference between Agile and Waterfall development life cycles.

infosys.name: Infosys
infosys.focus: Web technologies, database fundamentals (Normalization, Indexing), REST APIs, and basic software testing.
sampleQuestions: Explain the normalization process in relational databases up to Third Normal Form (3NF).
What are REST APIs? What are the key HTTP methods and response codes you would use?
What is the difference between black-box testing and white-box testing?

zoho.name: Zoho
zoho.focus: C/Java dry runs, custom recursion, pattern printing, custom array/string algorithms without using standard library functions, L2 Object-Oriented design (e.g. Call Taxi booking, Railway Reservation), and L3 System Design.
sampleQuestions: Design a Call Taxi Booking System with linear points A-F, booking allocation based on nearest free taxi or lowest earnings in case of a tie, and details tracking.
Write a logic in Java/C to print a 2D matrix in spiral or zig-zag fashion, or checking if the version string comparison represents an upgrade or downgrade.
Explain how you would find the first occurrence of all characters of a second string in a first string, printing characters between the lowest and highest index.
How would you solve the MS-Paint Flood Fill algorithm to replace the color of a given pixel and all adjacent same-colored pixels?

adobe.name: Adobe
adobe.focus: Advanced data structures (trees, tries, graphs), low-level system performance, graphics/rendering optimizations, and Object-Oriented Design (OOD).
sampleQuestions: Explain how you would optimize a polygon rendering algorithm to reduce memory usage during graphics processing.
How would you design a real-time collaborative whiteboarding tool that synchronizes updates across thousands of concurrent clients?
Explain the time and space complexity difference between using a Trie vs a HashMap for autocomplete search suggestions.

cisco.name: Cisco
cisco.focus: TCP/IP networking stacks, protocol implementations, operating systems fundamentals (processes, memory management), concurrency, and C/C++ systems development.
sampleQuestions: What is the difference between TCP and UDP at the socket level? Under what scenario would you write a custom application-layer protocol on UDP?
Explain how a router handles package header validation and checksum checks at wire speed.
What are the deadlock prevention techniques in operating systems, and how would you implement a lock-ordering system?

paypal.name: PayPal
paypal.focus: Transactional integrity (ACID), API rate limiting, distributed payment orchestration, and database concurrency controls.
sampleQuestions: How would you ensure double-spend prevention in a highly concurrent distributed payments API under network partition?
What is your approach to handling multi-currency conversion transactions with strict decimal precision and roundoff compliance?
Design a token bucket rate limiter to protect payment processing APIs from sudden traffic spikes.

walmart.name: Walmart
walmart.focus: E-commerce inventory scaling, order-fulfillment pipeline architectures, message brokers (Kafka), and cloud-native microservices.
sampleQuestions: Design a real-time global stock-level tracker that updates product availability instantly across millions of store-front page views.
How do you structure event-driven messaging queues to guarantee order delivery while processing transactions asynchronously?
Explain how you would handle read-heavy caching strategies for product catalog search results during a peak flash sale.

accenture.name: Accenture
accenture.focus: Enterprise integration patterns, REST/SOAP APIs, software development life cycle (SDLC) models, and client consulting situational readiness.
sampleQuestions: Explain the difference between SOAP and REST web services, and how you would migrate a legacy SOAP service to REST.
How would you coordinate with client stakeholders to gather technical specifications for a large-scale database migration project?
What is the difference between functional, integration, and user acceptance testing (UAT) in enterprise delivery?

general.name: General Mock
general.focus: General computer science fundamentals, coding problem-solving, and communication skills.
sampleQuestions: Tell me about a complex project you recently worked on. What were the technical challenges you faced?
What are your core strengths as a software developer, and how do you keep yourself updated with new tech?
